profile.timingNotes: Take on an empty stomach. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Preclinical models demonstrate that tissue repair and angiogenic stimulation (VEGFR2 upregulation) peak within 8 weeks. Extending cycles beyond this duration shows diminishing returns for acute soft tissue healing.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout period is recommended to prevent receptor habituation, clear active peptide residues, and assess baseline tissue healing progress.

citations.title: Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and Wound Healing

profile.timingNotes: Subcutaneous injection, can be taken anytime. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 6-week cycle is sufficient to promote cellular migration (fibroblasts and keratinocytes via G-actin binding) during active healing phases. Avoids prolonged exposure to angiogenic stimulants.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout allows cell motility pathways to return to baseline and prevents downregulation of angiogenesis response signaling.

citations.title: Thymosin beta-4: a multi-functional regenerative peptide. Basic properties and clinical applications

profile.timingNotes: Inject once weekly on the same day each week, with or without food.
profile.cycleRationale: Intended for continuous, chronic administration to achieve sustained glycemic control and appetite suppression. Discontinuation leads to rebound hunger and weight regain.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No planned washout period; continuous GLP-1 receptor activation is required to maintain metabolic improvements, with clinical trials supporting long-term safety.

citations.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity

profile.timingNotes: Inject once weekly on the same day each week, with or without food.
profile.cycleRationale: Intended for chronic, long-term administration. Dual activation of GIP and GLP-1 receptors requires sustained presence to reset metabolic setpoints and maintain weight loss.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No planned washout period is indicated. Continuous therapy is necessary for long-term weight management and glycemic stability, backed by clinical trial safety data.

citations.title: Tirzepatide Once Weekly for the Treatment of Obesity

profile.timingNotes: Subcutaneous injection, can cause local injection site pain. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 6-week cycle is standard for collagen synthesis and tissue remodeling. Limits cumulative systemic copper accumulation and local injection site irritation.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week rest period allows systemic copper levels to normalize and prevents intracellular copper transport saturation.

citations.title: Regenerative and Protective Actions of the GHK-Cu Peptide in the Light of the New Gene Data

profile.timingNotes: Inject subcutaneously once daily into the abdomen; rotate injection sites.
profile.cycleRationale: Usually administered continuously for the treatment of HIV-associated lipodystrophy, as discontinuation leads to a rapid reversion of visceral adipose tissue reduction.
profile.restPeriodRationale: Continuous administration is required to maintain growth hormone release and reduce abdominal fat; clinical studies show no rebound tachyphylaxis but immediate loss of efficacy upon cessation.

profile.timingNotes: Inject in the morning to align with pineal rhythms. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Short, intensive 10-14 day (2-week) courses are sufficient to stimulate telomerase activity and restore pineal melatonin secretion, as observed in clinical trials.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A long 6-month (24-week) rest period is recommended as the telomere elongation and anti-aging benefits persist long-term, and frequent cycles are unnecessary and lack long-term safety data.

profile.timingNotes: Inject in the morning, prior to physical activity. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.

profile.restPeriodRationale: An 8-week rest period prevents cellular adaptation to exogenous mitochondrial-derived peptides and allows tracking of endogenous metabolic adaptation.

citations.title: The Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide MOTS-c Promotes Metabolic Homeostasis and Reduces Obesity and Insulin Resistance

profile.timingNotes: Subcutaneous or oral administration for anti-inflammatory action. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.

profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout allows evaluation of immune system homeostasis and prevents potential desensitization of melanocortin receptor pathways.

profile.restPeriodRationale: An 8-week washout period is recommended to assess persistent neuroprotective benefits and avoid desensitizing cell survival pathways.

citations.title: ARA 290 for treatment of small fiber neuropathy in sarcoidosis

profile.restPeriodRationale: No washout period is scheduled; chronic treatment is required to sustain metabolic setpoint modifications and satiety signals.

profile.restPeriodRationale: A 24-week (6-month) washout is required because senescent cell clearance is a durable, long-term cellular reset, and frequent clearing is unnecessary and toxic to normal tissue regeneration.
